MATTIE & LANDRY JOHNSON
MAY 24 – JUNE 12, 2027
DFW → BARCELONA → MIAMI → DFW
2 GUESTS · 20 NIGHTS
Next: Your Trip at a Glance
Mattie & Landry — welcome to the trip of a lifetime. This proposal walks you through every detail of your 20-night journey: three nights in Barcelona, thirteen nights aboard the brand-new Norwegian Aura crossing the Atlantic, and two nights on Miami Beach before heading home. Scroll down through each section at your own pace.

INCLUDED IN YOUR NCL PACKAGE

Next: Barcelona & Hotel Options
INTERNATIONAL · PASSPORT REQUIRED · SPAIN
Barcelona
& Catalunya
Barcelona in late May is perfection — warm evenings, pre-peak crowds, and the city at its most golden. I've selected three boutique hotels in the Eixample, all within walking distance of Gaudí's masterworks. Tap the big black price bar below each hotel to select it — it'll turn green when chosen.

CURRENCY / VISAEuro (€)No visa required · ETIAS authorization needed by 2027

A stunning year-round rooftop pool with city panoramas anchors this 5-star boutique, 10 minutes on foot from Gaudí's masterworks. The Michelin-starred Caelis is on-site.

AGENT'S NOTE

Ohla delivers excellent value in this tier — the rooftop pool alone is worth it after long days walking. Starting price based on the quoted Superior Double room class; final rate confirmed at booking.

Architect-owned and art-infused, every room features a unique work by a local artist. Rooftop panoramas, the celebrated Virens restaurant, and steps from Gaudí and Gucci.

AGENT'S NOTE

Almanac is an exceptional mid-range choice — the design details and Virens alone set it apart. Starting price based on the quoted Deluxe Double; final rate confirmed at booking.

One of Barcelona's few remaining private garden hotels in a landmark 1860 building. Hammam spa, heated pool, fingerprint room entry — understated luxury at its finest.

AGENT'S NOTE

Alma is the most private and serene of the three — ideal if the Johnsons want a true retreat. Slightly above the original target, but worth every penny. Starting price based on quoted room class; final rate confirmed at booking.

BARCELONA ITINERARY — MAY 25–28
DAY 1May 25
Arrival & The Gothic Quarter
Arrive at BCN and check in to your hotel. Slow afternoon stroll through the Barri Gòtic — cobblestone lanes, Roman ruins, and the magnificent Barcelona Cathedral. Let the evening unfold with cava and tapas on a sidewalk terrace.
DAY 2May 26
Gaudí Day & Dinner at Lasarte
Pre-booked morning at the Sagrada Família. Afternoon at Casa Batlló or La Pedrera on Passeig de Gràcia. Evening at Lasarte (3 Michelin stars) — Chef Martín Berasategui's crown jewel.
DAY 3May 27
Barceloneta, Picasso & Last Evening
Morning at the Picasso Museum in El Born, then Barceloneta Beach for a long Mediterranean lunch. Afternoon — Montjuïc by cable car, the Boqueria market, or a spa treatment. Final evening tapas crawl through El Born.
DAY 4May 28
Embarkation — Norwegian Aura
Relaxed morning checkout, then transfer to Port of Barcelona (~20 min) to board the brand-new Norwegian Aura for her inaugural transatlantic crossing.
AGENT'S NOTE — BARCELONA

ETIAS authorization will be required for US passport holders by 2027 — apply online well in advance. Sagrada Família tower tickets must be reserved months ahead — non-negotiable. Roundtrip DFW flights and ground transportation are bundled into the NCL package. Allow 45–60 minutes from Eixample to the port on embarkation morning.

Next: Norwegian Aura — The Crossing
NORWEGIAN AURA · INAUGURAL TRANSATLANTIC · 13 NIGHTS
Norwegian Aura
Transatlantic
Mattie & Landry, this is the Norwegian Aura's very first Atlantic crossing — and you have two exceptional Haven stateroom options to choose from. Both packages include flights, Free at Sea, NorwegianCare, and all taxes. The bundle prices shown are contingent on also selecting your Barcelona and Miami hotels. Tap the black price bar under the cabin that's right for you.

A refined stateroom with private balcony and full Haven enclave access — 24-hour butler, concierge, private sundeck, infinity pool, and exclusive restaurant. Ideal for two.

AGENT'S NOTE

The Penthouse is the most compelling value in The Haven for a couple — you get the same butler service, private dining, and sundeck access as larger suites at a lower bundle price. Starting price contingent on selecting Barcelona and Miami hotel options.

OPTION B — HAVEN FAMILY SUITE

Haven Family Suite

The largest Haven stateroom option — two separate bedrooms, a private living area, and a balcony with dining. All the same Haven enclave privileges: butler, concierge, private sundeck, infinity pool, and exclusive restaurant.

AGENT'S NOTE

The Family Suite is the space upgrade — two full bedrooms and a living area mean you never feel on top of each other during nine sea days. If the extra room matters, this is the one. Starting price contingent on selecting Barcelona and Miami hotel options.

INCLUDED IN BOTH CABIN PACKAGES
FLIGHTSRoundtrip DFWAirfare + ground transport included in bundle

NORWEGIANCARETravel ProtectionComprehensive coverage for both guests
BUTLER & CONCIERGE24-Hour HavenIn-suite dining, priority reservations, private parties
CRUISE ITINERARY — MAY 28 – JUNE 10, 2027
DAY 1May 28
Embarkation — Barcelona
Board at Port of Barcelona and settle into your Haven stateroom. Explore Ocean Heights, the Ocean Boulevard promenade, and The Haven's private sundeck and pool.
DAY 2May 29
Motril (Granada), Spain
Gateway to Granada and the incomparable Alhambra palace — use your included excursion credits here. A rare bucket-list opportunity.
DAY 3May 30
Cádiz (Seville), Spain
Consider the full-day excursion to Seville — the Cathedral, the Alcázar, and the Barrio Santa Cruz — or explore Cádiz with its golden cathedral dome.
DAY 4May 31
Gibraltar, U.K.
Cable car to the top of the Rock for views across to Africa, meet the Barbary macaques, explore St. Michael's Cave, and browse duty-free Main Street.
DAYS 5–13Jun 1–9
Nine Days at Sea — The Atlantic Crossing
The heart of the transatlantic. Haven guests enjoy exclusive pool deck, sundeck, restaurant, lounge, and bar. Use your 4 specialty dining meals at Cagney's or Onda by Scarpetta. Broadway-caliber entertainment nightly.
DAY 14Jun 10
Arrival — Miami, Florida
Sail into PortMiami. Haven guests receive priority disembarkation. Ground transport to your hotel is included.
AGENT'S NOTE — CRUISE

This is the Norwegian Aura's inaugural transatlantic crossing — Haven staterooms are extremely limited. Both packages include Risk-Free Cancellation and 2-For-1 Deposits for maximum flexibility. The Free at Sea excursion credits should be applied to the Alhambra excursion at Motril — book immediately upon boarding as it sells out fast. The 4 specialty dining meals are best used across the nine sea days.

Next: Miami Beach & Hotel Options
DOMESTIC · NO PASSPORT REQUIRED · FLORIDA
Miami Beach
& South Beach
After thirteen days at sea, Miami Beach is the perfect sun-soaked landing. I've lined up three hotels ranging from vibrant to refined to premium beachfront. Tap the black price bar under the one that calls to you.

Daily pool parties with DJs, complimentary bikes, vibrant outdoor pool, whimsically designed rooms in Miami's signature pastels. Steps to the beach. Prosecco on arrival.

The Goodtime is fun and energetic — a great way to decompress after thirteen days at sea. Starting price based on quoted room class.

Travel + Leisure world's best hotel. Art Deco landmark on Ocean Drive — nightly live jazz, rooftop pool, private beach access, and the celebrated LT Steak & Seafood.

The Betsy is my top pick for the Johnsons — refined without being stuffy, nightly jazz, and the most authentic Ocean Drive experience. Starting price based on quoted room class.

MIAMI ITINERARY — JUNE 10–12
DAY 1Jun 10
Arrival & Ocean Drive
Disembark the Aura at PortMiami and taxi to South Beach. After 13 days at sea, the first afternoon is about reacclimating — lunch on Ocean Drive, a slow walk through the Art Deco District, and a dip in the Atlantic.
DAY 2Jun 11
South Beach & Farewell Dinner
Breakfast at Puerto Sagua, rent bikes on Lincoln Road, afternoon at the beach. Visit Wynwood Walls then a farewell dinner at one of Miami's top tables.
DAY 3Jun 12
Departure — MIA to DFW
Leisurely checkout and one final Cuban coffee before heading to MIA. Allow 45–60 minutes from South Beach. Return flight to DFW included in your NCL package.
AGENT'S NOTE — MIAMI

June in Miami is warm and humid with occasional afternoon showers — mornings and evenings are perfect. Carbone and Zuma book out weeks in advance — advise Mattie and Landry to lock reservations before the cruise departs. Return flight to DFW is included in the NCL package.

Next: Dining & Attractions Worth Exploring
Explore More
Here are some additional restaurants and attractions across your trip. Tap any that interest you — they'll turn green and we'll keep track of your picks.
DINING — BARCELONA
Lasarte
3 Michelin Stars · Basque-Catalan tasting menu · ~€180–€220/pp
Disfrutar
3 Michelin Stars · El Bulli alumni · Legendary tasting · ~€190–€215/pp
Mont Bar
1 Michelin Star · Refined tapas, relaxed atmosphere · ~€60–€90/pp
ATTRACTIONS — BARCELONA
Sagrada Família
Book tower access for most dramatic experience · Reserve 60+ days ahead · ~€32–€47/pp
Casa Batlló or La Pedrera
Evening "Magic Nights" especially recommended at La Pedrera · ~€35–€40/pp
Picasso Museum
El Born neighborhood · One of the world's finest Picasso collections · ~€15/pp
DINING — MIAMI BEACH
Carbone Miami
South Beach icon · Italian-American · Book weeks ahead · ~$85–$130/pp
Zuma Miami
Japanese izakaya-style · Downtown waterfront · Exceptional sake · ~$90–$140/pp
KYU Miami
Asian-inspired wood-fired BBQ · Design District · ~$65–$95/pp
ATTRACTIONS — MIAMI BEACH
Wynwood Walls
World's premier outdoor street art museum · Free to walk; ticketed indoor · ~$0–$12/pp
Art Deco Historic District Tour
Miami Design Preservation League guided walk · ~$30/pp
South Beach & Lummus Park
Beach chairs, volleyball, lifeguard stands — the full Miami Beach experience · ~$15–$20 for chairs
